Name: curvature_aware_precision_optimization_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision-Optimization Routing: A Unified Framework for Efficient Inference and Stable Optimization
Short Hypothesis: The curvature of the loss landscape serves as a unified proxy for both precision sensitivity and optimization trajectory stability, enabling a training-free framework that dynamically allocates precision and guides optimization updates.
Related Work: Related work includes Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR), which focuses on precision allocation based on activation geometry, and Spectral Latent Steering (SLS), which uses spectral projections for test-time optimization. CAPOR distinguishes itself by integrating precision allocation and optimization efficiency into a unified framework using curvature diagnostics.
Abstract: We propose Curvature-Aware Precision-Optimization Routing (CAPOR), a training-free framework that leverages curvature diagnostics to dynamically allocate precision and guide optimization updates. CAPOR hypothesizes that the curvature of the loss landscape serves as a unified proxy for both precision sensitivity and optimization trajectory stability. By analyzing curvature metrics (e.g., Hessian eigenvalues) in real-time, CAPOR allocates precision to layers or tokens based on their sensitivity to quantization noise and guides optimization updates to ensure stable convergence. We theoretically ground CAPOR in PAC-Bayes bounds and curvature-aware optimization theory, showing that curvature diagnostics provide a mechanistic link between computational efficiency and optimization stability. Empirically, we validate CAPOR on benchmark tasks, demonstrating superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers and faster convergence compared to static precision allocation and full-precision optimization baselines. CAPOR bridges precision routing and optimization theory, offering a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference and stable optimization.
Experiments: Curvature-Precision Mapping: Measure the correlation between curvature metrics (e.g., Hessian eigenvalues) and precision sensitivity across layers and tokens.
Optimization Efficiency: Compare curvature-guided optimization updates to full-precision updates in terms of convergence speed and final performance.
End-to-End Evaluation: Evaluate CAPOR on benchmark tasks (e.g., WILDS) to measure its impact on throughput, accuracy, and convergence stability.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Computational Overhead: Real-time curvature analysis may introduce overhead that offsets precision savings.
Sensitivity to Noise: Curvature estimates can be noisy, potentially leading to suboptimal precision allocation or optimization updates.
